summary: Cleanup pass for Pikachu/Pichu fighter symbols: many generic func_80124xxx-80128xxx names were renamed to ftPikachu_* names across C sources, headers, global function declarations, fighter data tables, Pichu/Pikachu action tables, and a small item assembly callsite. The PR mostly improved symbol readability and cross-file consistency rather than adding new behavior.
what_changed: Renamed numerous Pikachu functions from generic func_80124xxx-style symbols to ftPikachu_* names in ftpikachu2.c, ftpikachu3.c, ftpikachu4.c, and ftpikachu5.c.
Expanded src/melee/ft/chara/ftPikachu/ftpikachu.h from a small partial header into a broad declaration list for pika2, pika3, pika4, and pika5 functions.
Updated action table references in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpikachu.s and asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpichu.s from func_* pointers to renamed ftPikachu_* symbols.

Updated asm/melee/ft/ftdata.s special-move callback pointers such as ftPikachu_ActionChange_80124830, ftPikachu_ActionChange_8012489C, ftPikachu_ActionChange_80124E38, ftPikachu_ActionChangeZeroVel_80125DE8, ftPikachu_ActionChangeZeroVel_80125E60, and ftPikachu_ActionChange_801278BC/80127924.
Updated include/functions.h commented unknown declarations to use more descriptive ftPikachu names, including ActionChange, ActionChangeUpdateVel, CheckProperty, and SetState variants.
Updated item assembly callsites in asm/melee/it/code_8027CF30.s to call renamed Pikachu helpers such as ftPikachu_CheckProperty_801275CC, ftPikachu_SetState_8012764C, and ftPikachu_80127608.
Made minor cleanup inside C bodies while renaming, e.g. changing func_8006F238(fighterObj) == 0 to !func_8006F238(fighterObj) in ftpikachu2.c.
smart_moves: The rename was applied through all dependent assembly references, not just C definitions, which is essential when action tables and handwritten assembly still refer directly to function symbols.

Function names preserve the address suffix while adding semantic prefixes such as ActionChange, Stub, EfSpawn, ZeroVelocity, UpdateVel, CheckProperty, SetState, and PrepCallActionChange. This keeps symbols searchable by old address while improving meaning.
Pichu action-table references were updated to point to the same renamed ftPikachu routines, preserving the shared Pikachu/Pichu implementation relationship visible in the diff.
The header cleanup groups declarations by source split comments like pika2, pika3, pika4, and pika5, making later decomp cleanup easier to navigate.
decomp_lessons: When renaming matched functions, update every symbol consumer: C definitions, local headers, global include declarations, fighter data tables, character action tables, and unrelated assembly callsites.
Address-suffixed descriptive names are useful during incremental decompilation because they improve readability without losing the ability to correlate back to map addresses and old func_* names.
Shared fighter code can require updates in more than one character table; here both ftpikachu.s and ftpichu.s had the same block of callback pointer renames.
Cleanup PRs can reduce future matching friction by converting anonymous symbols into role-oriented names before deeper behavioral documentation is available.
Headers should be brought up to date with renamed C symbols so future files can include the proper declarations instead of depending on broad or stale func_* declarations.
naming_conventions: Use ftPikachu_ as the subsystem/function prefix for Pikachu fighter routines.
Use ftPikachu_ActionChange_<address> for routines that perform Fighter_ActionStateChange-style transitions or are used as special action-change callbacks.
Use ftPikachu_Stub_<address> for empty/no-op callback functions.
Use ftPikachu_EfSpawn_<address> for routines that spawn effects.
Use ftPikachu_ActionChangeZeroVel_<address> when the action change also zeroes or resets velocity.
Use ftPikachu_ActionChangeUpdateVel_<address> for action-change helpers that update velocity.
Use ftPikachu_CheckProperty_<address> and ftPikachu_SetState_<address> where the diff evidence indicates property checks or state-setting behavior.
Keep the original address suffix in renamed symbols, e.g. ftPikachu_ActionChange_80124E38, to preserve traceability.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed symbols in .4byte function-pointer tables without altering the table layout, preserving binary/data structure intent while improving symbol names.
Updated direct branch/call annotations in item assembly to renamed symbols while keeping the underlying branch offsets and assembly content otherwise stable.
Kept Pichu and Pikachu assembly tables in sync when both tables referenced the same callback sequence.
No evidence in the PR excerpt of new nonmatching-to-matching C conversions; this appears primarily to be symbol cleanup and reference propagation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only says: "Done - OK - ready for merge".
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that give semantic names to remaining ftPikachu_<address> functions without descriptive middle terms.
Check whether include/functions.h retained stale commented Pikachu prototypes after the dedicated ftpikachu.h declarations were expanded.
Search for remaining func_80124 through func_801283 references to verify Pikachu symbol cleanup completion.
Compare later ftPikachu naming conventions for OnDeath, OnItem, LoadSpecialAttrs, and special-move callbacks against this PR's ActionChange/Stub/EfSpawn pattern.
